model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Illinois is one of the most dominant teams in the bracket, ranking 6th-7th nationally with the #1 adjusted offense in the country (AdjO 131.2) and an adjusted efficiency margin of +32.14, a staggering 11+ points per 100 possessions higher than North Carolina's +20.86. The Tar Heels are critically weakened by the season-ending loss of star freshman Caleb Wilson—their leading scorer (19.8 ppg) and rebounder (9.4 rpg), a projected top-5 NBA pick—leaving UNC without the frontcourt anchor needed to match Illinois's elite offensive firepower. While North Carolina's ACC pedigree and 5-3 record without Wilson show they can compete, the massive quality gap combined with Illinois's historically potent offense should overwhelm UNC's solid but unspectacular defense (AdjD 100.6, 34th nationally) in a matchup that heavily favors the Illini.

reasoningStep.title: Illinois's elite offense and UNC's Wilson absence create decisive gap
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois holds an 11+ point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over a North Carolina team missing its best player, making this a comfortable pick for the 3-seed despite UNC's tournament pedigree.
evidence: Illinois ranks KenPom #7 (AdjEM +32.14) with the nation's #1 offense (AdjO 131.2) and a solid 24th-ranked defense (AdjD 98.1-99.1)
North Carolina ranks KenPom #29 (AdjEM +20.86) with a 33rd-ranked offense (AdjO 121.5) and 34th-ranked defense (AdjD 100.0-100.6)
UNC is without Caleb Wilson (season-ending broken right thumb), who averaged 19.8 ppg and 9.4 rpg as a projected top-5 NBA pick; UNC went 5-3 without him
Illinois lost a competitive OT game to Wisconsin 91-88 in the Big Ten Tournament quarterfinal but enters at full strength with no significant injury concerns
The 11+ point efficiency margin gap per 100 possessions is significantly wider than the typical 3-vs-6 seed matchup, heavily favoring Illinois
